name: Molasses Candy
recipeInstructions: An iron kettle with a rounded bottom (Scotch kettle) or copper kettle is best for candy making. If you don't have a copper kettle, use a granite kettle. Place the butter in the kettle and put it over fire. When melted, add molasses and sugar. Stir until the sugar is dissolved. During the initial boiling phase, stirring is not necessary, but once nearly cooked, stir constantly. Boil until, when dropped into cold water, the mixture becomes brittle. Add vinegar just before removing from heat. Pour into a well-buttered pan. When cool enough to handle, pull until porous and light-colored, allowing contact with the tips of fingers and thumbs without squeezing in the hand. Cut into small pieces using large shears or a sharp knife, then arrange on slightly buttered plates to cool.
